entries.schema: { providers: ProviderConfig[]; activeProviderId: string | null }; at most 20 providers
entries.legacyLimit: 20 records; byte size is not bounded
references: provider.id -> sessions/provider usage
activeProviderId -> providers[].id
entries.recovery: Parse failure resets the in-memory list. Phase 2 must quarantine the corrupt file and migrate from a pre-write backup.

entries.schema: { sessions: SessionData[] }; task, tool runtime and agent runtime are embedded
entries.legacyLimit: 50 sessions; message and embedded runtime byte size is not bounded
references: session.id -> attachments
session.id -> session-runtime
session.id -> executions
entries.recovery: Atomic replacement is already used. Parse failure currently returns an empty list; Phase 2 must preserve and quarantine the original.

entries.schema: GeneralSettings with default-based field migration
entries.legacyLimit: byte size is not bounded
references: subAgentModels[].providerId -> providers[].id
entries.recovery: Parse failure restores defaults. Phase 2 must quarantine the invalid file before writing defaults.

entries.schema: { projects: RecentProject[] }
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: project.id is consumed by sessions and UI workspace state
entries.recovery: Missing or invalid data becomes an empty list; Phase 2 must preserve a corrupt copy.

entries.schema: { rules: PermissionRule[] }
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: workspace and operation scopes refer to normalized paths and tool identities
entries.recovery: Invalid data must fail closed; retain the source and start with no allow rules.

entries.schema: Record<canonicalWorkspacePath, PermissionMode>
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: keys are canonical workspace paths
entries.recovery: Invalid data must fall back to auto mode and preserve the source for recovery.

entries.schema: redacted PermissionAuditEvent per line
entries.legacyLimit: byte size and retention are not bounded
references: session, tool call, rule and workspace identifiers when present
entries.recovery: Keep valid lines through the first malformed line and quarantine malformed suffixes; never replay decisions.

entries.schema: user MCP server definitions and enabled state
entries.legacyLimit: server count and byte size are not bounded
references: secret expressions -> mcp-secrets
server name -> OAuth and connection state
entries.recovery: Invalid user configuration is isolated; project and managed layers remain independently loadable.

entries.schema: trusted project configuration fingerprints
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: fingerprint -> project .mcp.json or .codez/mcp.local.json
entries.recovery: Invalid or missing entries are untrusted by default and must never be reconstructed as trusted.

entries.schema: encrypted Record<secretName, secretValue>
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: ${secret:NAME} expressions in MCP configuration
entries.recovery: Use the read-only legacy decryptor. Unavailable or corrupt ciphertext becomes requires_reentry; plaintext fallback is forbidden.

entries.schema: encrypted OAuth client and token records keyed by server
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: server name -> MCP configuration
entries.recovery: Use the read-only legacy decryptor. Decryption failures require reauthorization and cannot silently discard refresh state.

entries.schema: mcp-content:// handle, media type, byte length and immutable payload
entries.legacyLimit: 25 MiB per binary payload
readers: MCP content normalization and UI preview
references: mcp-content:// handle embedded in normalized MCP results
entries.recovery: Missing objects invalidate only their handles; orphaned objects may be removed after reference inventory.

entries.schema: AttachmentMetadata { attachment: ComposerImageAttachment; createdAt: number }
entries.legacyLimit: 100 MiB import safety limit; provider-specific request limits apply later
references: storageKey and sessionId embedded in SessionData messages
entries.recovery: Writes use temporary directories and rename. Remove stale drafts after 24 hours and session orphans only after live-session inventory.

entries.schema: versioned canonical model ledger records
entries.legacyLimit: byte size is not bounded
references: sessionId, turnId, messageId, toolCallId and scope identifiers
entries.recovery: Validate records and sequence, preserve conflict backups, and rebuild from the last valid snapshot plus suffix without replaying effects.

entries.schema: versioned compacted ledger snapshot and recovery cursors
entries.legacyLimit: byte size is not bounded
references: ledger sequence and session runtime reference in sessions.json
entries.recovery: Ignore an invalid snapshot and rebuild from a validated ledger; never replace a valid ledger with an older snapshot.

entries.format: JSON metadata plus original file bytes
entries.schema: transaction metadata with path, SHA-256, mode and expected post-mutation state
entries.legacyLimit: transaction count and byte size are not bounded
references: sessionId, transactionId and absolute workspace file paths
entries.recovery: Atomic metadata writes; an incomplete transaction stays recoverable and must require fingerprint verification before rollback.

entries.schema: redacted ToolJournalEvent
entries.legacyLimit: 10 MiB per file, 5 files, 30 day rotated-file age
references: sessionId, turnId, callId, schema fingerprint and permission rule id
entries.recovery: Skip malformed lines without replaying tools; rotation files are independent evidence segments.

entries.schema: ToolResultMetadata with handle, hashes, byte counts and SHA-256
entries.legacyLimit: persisted payload is not bounded; reads are capped at 50,000 characters
references: tool-result:// handle in ledger/tool results
entries.recovery: Verify metadata ownership and content SHA-256; missing pairs invalidate only the handle and must not affect other results.

entries.schema: ParallelExecutionSnapshot with sequence and control epoch
entries.legacyLimit: execution count and byte size are not bounded
references: sessionId, plan slug, task step id, worktree and executor ids
entries.recovery: Corrupt snapshots are isolated. Nonterminal executors recover as lost/decision_required and are never automatically replayed.

entries.schema: Plan metadata plus ordered PlanStep sections
entries.legacyLimit: plan count and byte size are not bounded
references: projectId, workspace hash, plan slug and task step ids
entries.recovery: One malformed plan is skipped without affecting other plans; source Markdown remains user recoverable.

entries.schema: enabled/disabled skill identifiers by global or workspace scope
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: skill id -> builtin, external or workspace skill directories
entries.recovery: Invalid configuration falls back to discovered defaults while preserving the source for inspection.

entries.schema: project fingerprint -> ProjectSnapshot
entries.legacyLimit: record and byte size are not bounded
references: workspace paths and package fingerprints
entries.recovery: Disposable cache: invalid or stale entries are deleted and rebuilt from the workspace.

entries.schema: Rules, memory entries and front matter defined by RulesResolver and MemoryService
entries.legacyLimit: file count and byte size are bounded only by workspace scanning limits
references: workspace root, rule path and prompt source identifiers
entries.recovery: User-authored files are authoritative and never rewritten during discovery; malformed files are reported and skipped.
